name: Wiliwilinui Ridge
tagline: The Ko'olau's best-kept ridge secret — pass through a gated community into native forest with panoramic views.
parking: Limited spots — check in with security at the Waialae Iki gate on Okoa St
hours: Daylight hours only; security gate staffed roughly 7am–6pm
bestTime: Early morning for clear summit views (clouds roll in by midday)
talkStory: Named after the wiliwili tree (Erythrina sandwicensis), a native dryland species that once grew abundantly on these slopes before development pushed it to near-extinction. The trail was historically used by Hawaiian bird catchers who harvested feathers for royal capes and helmets — the upper ridge still harbors native 'apapane and 'amakihi. Check in with security at the Waialae Iki gate. The Ko'olau summit rewards with panoramic views of both coasts — 4.5 miles, 1,500 ft gain.
